revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=大宮神社| 所在地=和歌山県岩出市宮75| 位置=| 祭神=日本武尊| 社格=郷社| 創建=[[712年]]（[[和銅]]5年）| 例祭=10月第1土曜日（大宮祭）| 本殿=| 神事=| 画像=[[画像:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|280px]] }} [[画像:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|220px|拝殿]] '''大宮神社'''（おおみやじんじゃ）は[[和歌山県]][[岩出市]]にある[[神社]]。
==由緒== 西暦[[712年]]（[[和銅]]5年）に[[熱田神宮]]より[[日本武尊]]を勧請して創建され、当地の産土神となった。 中世、[[高野山]]を下った興教大師[[覚鑁]]が[[根来寺]]開創に際し、[[1142年]]に仏法の守護神として[[神祇官]]八神を祀り1000神余を勧請した。それにより総社権現または三部権現、総社明神という名称になり、相前後して[[鳥羽上皇]]の勅願所に定められ隆盛したが、天正年間の[[豊臣秀吉]]の根来寺攻めの兵火を受けて社殿を焼失。現在の社殿は[[江戸時代]]に[[徳川頼宣]]に再建されて以降ものである。 近代においては、[[明治6年]]に[[村社]]、[[大正12年]]3月27日に[[郷社]]に列格。
==祭神== *主祭神 - 日本武尊 *配祀神 - 神祇官八神（高皇産霊神、神皇産霊神、魂留産霊神、生産霊神、足産霊神、大宮賣神、御食津神、事代主神）、[[素戔嗚尊]]等十二柱の大神
==境内== [[鎮守の杜]]に覆われた2万m2の社域を有する。 *本社 *神楽殿 *御供所 *鐘楼 *摂社 **衣毘須大黒天社 ***正八幡宮 **住吉社 **春日社
==祭事== *大宮祭 - 10月第1土曜日の深夜から開催される。斎刺祭（よみさしまつり）とも。
==交通アクセス== *JR[[和歌山線]] [[岩出駅]] 徒歩7分
{{Commons|Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} {{shinto-stub}}
[[Category:岩出市|おおみやしんしや]] [[Category:郷社|おおみやしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の神社|おおみやしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=大宮神社| 所在地=和歌山県岩出市宮75| 位置=| 祭神=日本武尊| 社格=郷社| 創建=[[712年]]（[[和銅]]5年）| 例祭=10月第1土曜日（大宮祭）| 本殿=| 神事=| 画像=[[画像:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|280px]] }} [[画像:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|220px|拝殿]] '''大宮神社'''（おおみやじんじゃ）は[[和歌山県]][[岩出市]]にある[[神社]]。
==由緒== 西暦[[712年]]（[[和銅]]5年）に[[熱田神宮]]より[[日本武尊]]を勧請して創建され、当地の産土神となった。 中世、[[高野山]]を下った興教大師[[覚鑁]]が[[根来寺]]開創に際し、[[1142年]]に仏法の守護神として[[神祇官]]八神を祀り1000神余を勧請した。それにより総社権現または三部権現、総社明神という名称になり、相前後して[[鳥羽上皇]]の勅願所に定められ隆盛したが、天正年間の[[豊臣秀吉]]の根来寺攻めの兵火を受けて社殿を焼失。現在の社殿は[[江戸時代]]に[[徳川頼宣]]に再建されて以降ものである。 近代においては、[[明治6年]]に[[村社]]、[[大正12年]]3月27日に[[郷社]]に列格。
==祭神== *主祭神 - 日本武尊 *配祀神 - 神祇官八神（高皇産霊神、神皇産霊神、魂留産霊神、生産霊神、足産霊神、大宮賣神、御食津神、事代主神）、[[素戔嗚尊]]等十二柱の大神
==境内== [[鎮守の杜]]に覆われた2万平方メートルの社域を有する。 *本社 *神楽殿 *御供所 *鐘楼 *摂社 **衣毘須大黒天社 ***正八幡宮 **住吉社 **春日社
==祭事== *大宮祭 - 10月第1土曜日の深夜から開催される。斎刺祭（よみさしまつり）とも。
==交通アクセス== *JR[[和歌山線]] [[岩出駅]] 徒歩7分

[[Category:岩出市|おおみやしんしや]] [[Category:郷社|おおみやしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の神社|おおみやしんしや]]

==祭神== *主祭神 - [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] *配祀神 - [[神祇官]]八神（[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、魂留産霊神、[[天活玉命|生産霊神]]、足産霊神、大宮賣神、御食津神、[[事代主|事代主神]]）、[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]等十二柱の大神
==境内== [[鎮守の杜]]に覆われた2万平方メートルの社域を有する。 *本社 *神楽殿 *御供所 *鐘楼 *摂社 **衣毘須大黒天社 **正[[八幡宮]] **[[住吉社]] **[[春日社]]
==祭事== *大宮祭 - 10月第1土曜日の深夜から開催される。斎刺祭（よみさしまつり）とも。
==交通アクセス== {{Commons|Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} *JR[[和歌山線]] [[岩出駅]] 徒歩7分

{{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=大宮神社 | 画像=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[和歌山県]][[岩出市]]宮75 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[和銅]]5年（[[712年]]） | 例祭=10月第1土曜日（大宮祭） | 本殿= | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=大宮神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|拝殿]] '''大宮神社'''（おおみやじんじゃ）は[[和歌山県]][[岩出市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == [[和銅]]5年（[[712年]]）に[[熱田神宮]]より[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を勧請して創建され、当地の[[産土神]]となった。中世、[[高野山]][[金剛峯寺]]を下った興教大師[[覚鑁]]が[[根来寺]]開創に際し、[[康治]]元年（[[1142年]]）に仏法の[[守護神]]として[[神祇官]]八神を祀り1,000神余を勧請した。それにより総社権現または三部権現、総社明神という名称になり、相前後して[[鳥羽天皇|鳥羽上皇]]の[[勅願所]]に定められ隆盛した。しかし[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]による[[紀州征伐]]（根来寺攻め）の兵火を受けて社殿を焼失。現在の社殿は[[江戸時代]]に[[紀州藩]]主[[徳川頼宣]]によって再建されて以降ものである。 近代においては、[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に[[村社]]、[[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）3月27日に[[郷社]]に列格。 == 祭神 == *主祭神 - [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] *配祀神 - [[神祇官]]八神（[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、魂留産霊神、[[天活玉命|生産霊神]]、足産霊神、[[大宮売神|大宮賣神]]、[[御食津神]]、[[事代主|事代主神]]）、[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]等十二柱の大神 == 境内 == [[鎮守の杜]]に覆われた2万平方メートルの社域を有する。 *本社 *神楽殿 *御供所 *鐘楼 *摂社 **衣毘須大黒天社 **正[[八幡宮]] **[[住吉社]] **[[春日社]] == 祭事 == *大宮祭 - 10月第1土曜日の深夜から開催される。斎刺祭（よみさしまつり）とも。 == 交通アクセス == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[和歌山線]] [[岩出駅]] 徒歩約10分 *[[樽井駅]]前、[[和泉砂川駅|砂川駅]]前から[[和歌山バス那賀]]岩出駅前行きに乗車し、大宮停留所で下車、徒歩約5分 *[[岩出市巡回バス]]、[[紀の川コミュニティバス]]宮停留所で下車、徒歩約5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 大宮神社] - 和歌山県神社庁 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやしんしや}} [[Category:岩出市の建築物]] [[Category:岩出市の歴史]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:和歌山県の神社]]

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */ 両枠がきちんと揃っているナビゲーションテンプレートとカテゴリの間に枠のないスタブテンプレートが挟まれているのは見栄えが悪いので、{{Shinto-stub}}を{{神道 横}}の上に移動。

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Shrine | Name=Ōmiya Shrine | Image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | Location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | Coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | Annual festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines * Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine * Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwade]] [[Category:History of Iwade]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[ja:大宮神社]]

The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | Coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwade]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:History of Iwade]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] [[ja:大宮神社]]

{{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Shrine | Name=Ōmiya Shrine | image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | Coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwade]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Iwade]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] [[ja:大宮神社]]

The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Shrine | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Shrine | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Shrine | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)-qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Shrine | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)-qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Shrine | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)-qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)-qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)-qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)-qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)-qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama
