{{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 20qq}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}[[Category:qq]] During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (qq42 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}[[Category:11]] Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]][[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original {{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]] [[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwade]] [[Category:History of Iwade]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] [[ja:大宮神社]] [[sn:miya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[en:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}
