Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]] [[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwade]] [[Category:History of Iwade]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] [[ja:大宮神社]] [[sn:miya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[en:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}

{{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]] [[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwade]] [[Category:History of Iwade]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] [[ja:大宮神社]] [[sn:miya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[en:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=大宮神社 | 画像=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[和歌山県]][[岩出市]]宮75 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[和銅]]5年（[[712年]]） | 例祭=10月第1土曜日（大宮祭） | 本殿= | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=大宮神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|拝殿]] '''大宮神社'''（おおみやじんじゃ）は[[和歌山県]][[岩出市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == [[和銅]]5年（[[712年]]）に[[熱田神宮]]より[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を勧請して創建され、当地の[[産土神]]となった。中世、[[高野山]][[金剛峯寺]]を下った興教大師[[覚鑁]]が[[根来寺]]開創に際し、[[康治]]元年（[[1142年]]）に仏法の[[守護神]]として[[神祇官]]八神を祀り1,000神余を勧請した。それにより総社権現または三部権現、総社明神という名称になり、相前後して[[鳥羽天皇|鳥羽上皇]]の[[勅願所]]に定められ隆盛した。しかし[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]による[[紀州征伐]]（根来寺攻め）の兵火を受けて社殿を焼失。現在の社殿は[[江戸時代]]に[[紀州藩]]主[[徳川頼宣]]によって再建されて以降ものである。 近代においては、[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に[[村社]]、[[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）3月27日に[[郷社]]に列格。 == 祭神 == *主祭神 - [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] *配祀神 - [[神祇官]]八神（[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、魂留産霊神、[[天活玉命|生産霊神]]、足産霊神、[[大宮売神|大宮賣神]]、[[御食津神]]、[[事代主|事代主神]]）、[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]等十二柱の大神 == 境内 == [[鎮守の杜]]に覆われた2万平方メートルの社域を有する。 *本社 *神楽殿 *御供所 *鐘楼 *摂社 **衣毘須大黒天社 **正[[八幡宮]] **[[住吉社]] **[[春日社]] == 祭事 == *大宮祭 - 10月第1土曜日の深夜から開催される。斎刺祭（よみさしまつり）とも。 == 交通アクセス == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[和歌山線]] [[岩出駅]] 徒歩約10分 *[[樽井駅]]前、[[和泉砂川駅|砂川駅]]前から[[和歌山バス那賀]]岩出駅前行きに乗車し、大宮停留所で下車、徒歩約5分 *[[岩出市巡回バス]]、[[紀の川コミュニティバス]]宮停留所で下車、徒歩約5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 大宮神社] - 和歌山県神社庁 {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやしんしや}} [[Category:岩出市の建築物]] [[Category:岩出市の歴史]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:和歌山県の神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} {{神社 | 名称=大宮神社 | 画像=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[和歌山県]][[岩出市]]宮75 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 創建=[[和銅]]5年（[[712年]]） | 例祭=10月第1土曜日（大宮祭） | 本殿= | 神事= | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=大宮神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|拝殿]] '''大宮神社'''（おおみやじんじゃ）は[[和歌山県]][[岩出市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == [[和銅]]5年（[[712年]]）に[[熱田神宮]]より[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]を勧請して創建され、当地の[[産土神]]となった。中世、[[高野山]][[金剛峯寺]]を下った興教大師[[覚鑁]]が[[根来寺]]開創に際し、[[康治]]元年（[[1142年]]）に仏法の[[守護神]]として[[神祇官]]八神を祀り1,000神余を勧請した。それにより総社権現または三部権現、総社明神という名称になり、相前後して[[鳥羽天皇|鳥羽上皇]]の[[勅願所]]に定められ隆盛した。しかし[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]による[[紀州征伐]]（根来寺攻め）の兵火を受けて社殿を焼失。現在の社殿は[[江戸時代]]に[[紀州藩]]主[[徳川頼宣]]によって再建されて以降ものである。 近代においては、[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に[[村社]]、[[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）3月27日に[[郷社]]に列格。 == 祭神 == *主祭神 - [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] *配祀神 - [[神祇官]]八神（[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊神]]、[[カミムスビ|神皇産霊神]]、魂留産霊神、[[天活玉命|生産霊神]]、足産霊神、[[大宮売神|大宮賣神]]、[[御食津神]]、[[事代主|事代主神]]）、[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]等十二柱の大神 == 境内 == [[鎮守の杜]]に覆われた2万平方メートルの社域を有する。 *本社 *神楽殿 *御供所 *鐘楼 *摂社 **衣毘須大黒天社 **正[[八幡宮]] **[[住吉社]] **[[春日社]] == 祭事 == *大宮祭 - 10月第1土曜日の深夜から開催される。斎刺祭（よみさしまつり）とも。 == 交通アクセス == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[和歌山線]] [[岩出駅]] 徒歩約10分 *[[樽井駅]]前、[[和泉砂川駅|砂川駅]]前から[[和歌山バス那賀]]岩出駅前行きに乗車し、大宮停留所で下車、徒歩約5分 *[[岩出市巡回バス]]、[[紀の川コミュニティバス]]宮停留所で下車、徒歩約5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 大宮神社] - 和歌山県神社庁 {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやしんしや}} [[Category:岩出市の建築物]] [[Category:岩出市の歴史]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:和歌山県の神社]]

{{Cite sources|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Haiden | location=[[Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[Wakayama Prefecture]]. == History == Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[Atsuta Shrine]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Jingi-kan]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]] [[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwade]] [[Category:History of Iwade]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] [[ja:大宮神社]] [[sn:miya Shrine (Iwade City)]] [[en:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|大宮神社 (岩出市)|version=102283753|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The eight deities of the [[Jingi-kan]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - During the medieval period, [[Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[Emperor Toba]]. However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[Edo period]] under [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain. In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[1923]] (Taishō 12). == Deities == * Main deity - [[Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others. == Grounds == The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]] == Festivals == * Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai. == Access == * [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]] [[Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[Tarui Station]] or [[Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434451]] Wikidata name: Omiya-jinja, Iwade [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg | location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{Coord|34.254222222222225|N|135.31241666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwade|qid=Q579623}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== History ==
Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[:en:Kakuban|Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]].
However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain.
In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12).
== Deities ==
* Main deity - [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others.
== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.
== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Iwade Station|Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[:en:Tarui Station|Tarui Station]] or [[:en:Izumi-Sunagawa Station|Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes.

<!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--><!--Omiya Shrine (Iwade City)--> [[Category:Buildings in Iwade, Wakayama]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11434451]] Wikidata name: Omiya-jinja, Iwade [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg | location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{Coord|34.254222222222225|N|135.31241666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwade|WD=Q579623}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== History ==
Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[:en:Kakuban|Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]].
However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain.
In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12).
== Deities ==
* Main deity - [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others.
== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.
== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Iwade Station|Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[:en:Tarui Station|Tarui Station]] or [[:en:Izumi-Sunagawa Station|Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11434451]] Wikidata name: Omiya-jinja, Iwade [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg | location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{Coord|34.254222222222225|N|135.31241666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwade|WD=Q579623}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== History ==
Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[:en:Kakuban|Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]].
However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain.
In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12).
== Deities ==
* Main deity - [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others.
== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.
== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Iwade Station|Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[:en:Tarui Station|Tarui Station]] or [[:en:Izumi-Sunagawa Station|Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11434451]] Wikidata name: Omiya-jinja, Iwade [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg | location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{Coord|34.254222222222225|N|135.31241666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwade|WD=Q579623}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== History ==
Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[:en:Kakuban|Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]].
However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain.
In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12).
== Deities ==
* Main deity - [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others.
== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.
== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Iwade Station|Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[:en:Tarui Station|Tarui Station]] or [[:en:Izumi-Sunagawa Station|Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes.

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} {{wikidata link|Q11434451}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwade, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11434451]] Wikidata name: Omiya-jinja, Iwade [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg | location=[[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{Coord|34.254222222222225|N|135.31241666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwade|WD=Q579623}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Iwade City]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]].
== History ==
Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] from [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, [[:en:Kakuban|Kakuban]], the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]].
However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current structures were rebuilt during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] under [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the ruler of the Kii domain.
In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taishō 12).
== Deities ==
* Main deity - [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Nihon Takeru]] * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others.
== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.
== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Iwade Station|Iwade Station]] - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From [[:en:Tarui Station|Tarui Station]] or [[:en:Izumi-Sunagawa Station|Izumi-Sunagawa Station]], take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade]][[ja:大宮神社 (岩出市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11434451]] Wikidata name: Omiya-jinja, Iwade [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg | location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}, [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{Coord|34.254222222222225|N|135.31241666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}} | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwade|WD=Q579623}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Iwade City]], {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}} from [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, {{ill|Kakuban|en|Kakuban|WD=Q1676043|lt=Kakuban}}, the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba}}.
However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current structures were rebuilt during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, the ruler of the Kii domain.
In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} (Taishō 12).
== Deities ==
* Main deity - {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}} * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others.
== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.
== Access ==
* {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Iwade Station|en|Iwade Station|WD=Q2681167|lt=Iwade Station}} - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From {{ill|Tarui Station|en|Tarui Station|WD=Q7686909|lt=Tarui Station}} or {{ill|Izumi-Sunagawa Station|en|Izumi-Sunagawa Station|WD=Q857378|lt=Izumi-Sunagawa Station}}, take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes.

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg | location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}, [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{Coord|34.254222222222225|N|135.31241666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}} | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwade|WD=Q579623}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Iwade City]], {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}} from [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, {{ill|Kakuban|en|Kakuban|WD=Q1676043|lt=Kakuban}}, the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba}}.
However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current structures were rebuilt during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} under {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, the ruler of the Kii domain.
In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} (Taishō 12).
== Deities ==
* Main deity - {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}} * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others.
== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.
== Access ==
* {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Iwade Station|en|Iwade Station|WD=Q2681167|lt=Iwade Station}} - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From {{ill|Tarui Station|en|Tarui Station|WD=Q7686909|lt=Tarui Station}} or {{ill|Izumi-Sunagawa Station|en|Izumi-Sunagawa Station|WD=Q857378|lt=Izumi-Sunagawa Station}}, take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]]
[[Category:Omiya-jinja, Iwade}} * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3045 Ōmiya Shrine] - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association

In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} (Taishō 12).

* Main deity - {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others.
== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg | location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{Coord|34.254222222222225|N|135.31241666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwade|WD=Q579623|1=Iwade, Wakayama}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Iwade City]], {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} from [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, {{ill|Kakuban|en|Kakuban|WD=Q1676043|lt=Kakuban|1=Kakuban}}, the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba|1=Emperor Toba}}.
However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current structures were rebuilt during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} under {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, the ruler of the Kii domain.
In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} (Taishō 12).

== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.

{{Unreferenced|date=March 2011}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ōmiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja02ds3200.jpg | location={{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, [[Iwade City]] 75 Miya | coordinates={{Coord|34.254222222222225|N|135.31241666666668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} | Shrine Rank=Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year=[[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) | festival=1st Saturday of October (Ōmiya Festival) | Main hall= | Rituals= | Map={{location map|Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan|label=Ōmiya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwade|WD=Q579623|1=Iwade, Wakayama}} }} [[File:Iwade ohmiya-jinja03ds3872.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Ōmiya Shrine''' (大宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Iwade City]], {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
Ōmiya Shrine was founded in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 5 (712 CE) by enshrining {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} from [[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] as the local [[ubusuna deity]]. During the medieval period, {{ill|Kakuban|en|Kakuban|WD=Q1676043|lt=Kakuban|1=Kakuban}}, the founder of [[Negoro-ji]], brought 1,000 additional deities, including the eight gods of the [[Department of Divinities]], in [[:en:Kōji|Kōji]] 1 (1142 CE). The shrine was renamed Sōsha Gongen, Sanbu Gongen, or Sōsha Myōjin. It prospered as an [[Imperial prayer site]] during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba|1=Emperor Toba}}.
However, the shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Kii Campaign]] (1585) led by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current structures were rebuilt during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} under {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, the ruler of the Kii domain.
In modern times, the shrine was designated a [[Sonsha]] in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) and promoted to [[Gōsha]] status on March 27, {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}} (Taishō 12).
== Deities ==
* Main deity - {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} * Subordinate deities - The eight deities of the [[Department of Divinities]] (e.g., [[Takami Musubi|Takami Musubi]], [[Kami Musubi|Kami Musubi]], Tamatsume Musubi, [[Ame-no-Kaguyama|Ikutsume Musubi]], [[Ōmiyame-no-Kami|Ōmiyame-no-Kami]], [[Miketsu-no-Kami|Miketsu-no-Kami]], and [[Koto-shiro-nushi|Kotoshironushi]]) and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] among others.
== Grounds ==
The shrine covers a 20,000 square meter area surrounded by a [[chinju no mori]] (sacred grove). * Main shrine * Kagura hall * Offerings hall * Bell tower * Auxiliary shrines ** Ebisu Daikokuten Shrine ** Sei [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] ** [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] ** [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]
== Festivals ==
* Ōmiya Festival - Held on the night of the first Saturday in October, also known as Yomisasai.
== Access ==
* {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Iwade Station|en|Iwade Station|WD=Q2681167|lt=Iwade Station|1=Iwade Station}} - Approx. 10-minute walk. * From {{ill|Tarui Station|en|Tarui Station|WD=Q7686909|lt=Tarui Station|1=Tarui Station}} or {{ill|Izumi-Sunagawa Station|en|Izumi-Sunagawa Station|WD=Q857378|lt=Izumi-Sunagawa Station|1=Izumi-Sunagawa Station}}, take a [[Wakayama Bus Naga]] to Iwade Station and alight at Ōmiya Stop, then walk 5 minutes. * [[Iwade City Circulation Bus]] and [[Kinokawa Community Bus]] - Alight at Miya Stop, then walk 5 minutes.
